cuisines.title: Al Pastor Tacos
cuisines.description: Thinly sliced marinated pork shaved from a vertical spit, served on small corn tortillas with pineapple, onion, and cilantro.
cuisines.funFact: The vertical spit method was actually brought to Mexico by Lebanese immigrants!

cuisines.title: Pad Thai
cuisines.description: Stir-fried rice noodles with eggs, tofu, tamarind pulp, fish sauce, dried shrimp, garlic, red chili pepper and palm sugar.
cuisines.funFact: Pad Thai was popularized in the 1930s to promote Thai nationalism and reduce rice consumption.

cuisines.title: Poutine
cuisines.description: A classic Canadian dish consisting of crispy french fries and fresh cheese curds, all smothered in a rich, savory brown gravy.
cuisines.funFact: The name 'Poutine' is Quebec slang for 'a mess', but it's a very delicious mess!

cuisines.title: Banh Mi
cuisines.description: A vibrant sandwich featuring a crusty baguette filled with various meats (like pork belly or pâté), pickled carrots, daikon, cilantro, and chili.
cuisines.funFact: The baguette was introduced to Vietnam during the colonial period, but locals made it lighter and crispier using rice flour.

cuisines.title: Currywurst
cuisines.description: A steamed then fried pork sausage typically cut into slices and seasoned with curry ketchup, topped with curry powder.
cuisines.funFact: About 800 million currywursts are eaten in Germany every year, with 70 million consumed in Berlin alone!

cuisines.title: Falafel
cuisines.description: Deep-fried balls or patties made from ground chickpeas or fava beans, typically served in a pita with salad and tahini sauce.
cuisines.funFact: While widely associated with the Middle East, many historians believe falafel originated in Egypt using fava beans.

cuisines.title: Artisan Gelato
cuisines.description: A frozen dessert of Italian origin, made with a base of butterfat, milk, and sugar, and flavored with fruit and nut purees.
cuisines.funFact: Gelato is served about 10-15 degrees warmer than American ice cream, which makes it feel creamier.
